title: Velvety Earthy Brazilian Chicken Liver Soup
description: A silky, earthy soup inspired by the flavors of Brazil, featuring tender chicken livers, roasted tomatoes, and coconut milk for a velvety finish, with a vegan option using jackfruit.

ingredients.name: Chicken liver
ingredients.notes: trimmed and cut into 1 cm pieces; for vegan option replace with 400 g shredded jackfruit

instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add diced onion and minced garlic, sauté until softened, about 3 minutes.
Add diced carrot and cubed potato, cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Stir in chopped tomato and a pinch of curry powder (optional), cooking until the tomatoes break down, about 3 minutes.
Pour in the chicken broth, bring to a gentle simmer, reduce heat to low, and simmer until the vegetables are tender, about 10 minutes.
Add the trimmed chicken liver (or shredded jackfruit) and simmer gently for 6–8 minutes until the liver is cooked through.
Using an immersion blender or a regular blender in batches, whisk the soup until it becomes silky and smooth; avoid over-blending to keep a slight texture.
Stir in coconut milk and warm gently over low heat; do not let it boil to preserve the coconut flavor.
Season with salt, pepper, and a pinch of smoked paprika for depth of flavor.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh cilantro, and optionally drizzle a splash of coconut cream.
